content.problemStatement:
Chef is a farmer and a pet lover. He has a lot of his favorite pets cats and dogs in the barn. He knows that there are CCC cats and DDD dogs in the barn. Also, one day went to field and found that there were LLL legs of the animals touching the ground. Chef knows that cats love to ride on the dogs. So, they might ride on the dogs, and their legs won't touch the ground and Chef would miss counting their legs. Chef's dogs are strong enough to ride at max two cats on their back.

It was a cold foggy morning, when Chef did this counting. So he is now wondering whether he counted the legs properly or not. Specifically, he is wondering whether it is possible that he counted correctly. Please help Chef in finding it.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, denoting the number of test cases. Each test case consists of a single line containing three space-separated integers C, D, and L, where C is the number of cats, D is the number of dogs, and L is the number of legs of animals counted by Chef.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single line containing a string 'yes' or 'no', according to whether it is possible that Chef counted the legs correctly.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Explanation: For the first test case, there is one cat and one dog. The number of legs of these animals on the ground are 8, it can be possible when both cat and dog are standing on the ground. For the second test case, there is one cat and one dog. The number of legs of these animals on the ground are 4, it can be possible if the cat will ride on the dog, so its legs won't be counted by Chef, only the dog's legs will be counted. For the third test case, there is one cat and one dog. The number of legs of these animals are 2, it can not be true at all, Chef might have made some mistake. Hence, the answer is no.

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we need to consider all possible scenarios of cats and dogs and their legs. Each cat has 4 legs and each dog has 4 legs. However, a cat can ride on a dog, in which case the cat's legs will not be counted. We need to check if the given number of legs can be achieved with the given number of cats and dogs.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) — we iterate over each test case once
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) — we use a constant amount of space to store the input and output
